---
title: "Equator Geospatial"
title-block-banner: false
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.eg-page}

::: {.eg-hero .eg-hero-dark}
::: {.eg-float-layer}
[NDVI = (NIR − Red) / (NIR + Red)]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-eq .eg-float-1}
[import ee]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-2}
[ee.ImageCollection('COPERNICUS/S2_SR')]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-3}
[∇·F = ρ]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-eq .eg-float-4}
[.filterDate('2020-01-01', '2020-12-31')]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-5}
[σ² = Σ(xᵢ − μ)² / n]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-eq .eg-float-6}
[class RandomForestClassifier:]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-7}
[λ = 0.05]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-eq .eg-float-8}
[gdal.Open(raster_path)]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-9}
[y = β₀ + β₁x + ε]{.eg-float-item .eg-float-eq .eg-float-10}
:::

::: {.eg-wrap}
[Geospatial Data Science · East Africa]{.eg-eyebrow}

# Independent geospatial science for East Africa's land, climate, and conservation decisions.

::: {.eg-lede}
Equator Geospatial is a Nairobi-based consultancy combining remote sensing, ecological modeling, and social-ecological systems research — built for donors, conservancies, and governments who need land-use and climate claims to hold up to scrutiny.
:::

::: {.eg-hero-ctas}
[Explore our services](services-geospatial.qmd#services){.eg-btn .eg-btn-solid} [Start a project](#contact){.eg-btn .eg-btn-outline}
:::

::: {.eg-city-strip}
NAIROBI · KAMPALA · DAR ES SALAAM · KIGALI · ADDIS ABABA
:::
:::
:::

::: {#about .eg-section}
::: {.eg-wrap}
[Who we are]{.eg-eyebrow}

## Science-first, not GIS-generalist.

::: {.eg-intro-lede}
We're a team of geospatial data scientists, ecologists, and social-ecological systems researchers — not a generalist mapping shop. Every project we take on sits at the intersection of remote sensing and real ecological and social science, which is exactly the layer most GIS-for-hire firms skip.
:::

::: {.eg-chip-row}
[Remote Sensing]{.eg-chip} [Landscape & Forest Ecology]{.eg-chip} [Spatiotemporal Modeling]{.eg-chip} [Ecological Modeling]{.eg-chip} [Land-Use Mapping & Modeling]{.eg-chip} [Social-Ecological Systems]{.eg-chip}
:::
:::
:::

::: {#what-we-do .eg-section .eg-services}
::: {.eg-wrap}
::: {.eg-sec-head}
[What we do]{.eg-eyebrow}

## Five layers of analysis — one landscape.

A quick look at the stack. Each layer is available on its own, or combined into a full landscape assessment.
:::

::: {.eg-teaser-grid}

::: {.eg-teaser-card}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l1}

[LAYER 01]{.eg-layer-index}

### Digital MRV & Carbon Verification

[Independent, satellite-grounded MRV for carbon and biodiversity credit projects.]{.eg-teaser-desc}

[Learn more →](services-geospatial.qmd#services){.eg-teaser-link}
:::

::: {.eg-teaser-card}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l2}

[LAYER 02]{.eg-layer-index}

### Land-Use & Land-Cover Change Modeling

[Mapping and forecasting how agriculture, settlement, and forest cover are moving.]{.eg-teaser-desc}

[Learn more →](services-geospatial.qmd#services){.eg-teaser-link}
:::

::: {.eg-teaser-card}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l3}

[LAYER 03]{.eg-layer-index}

### Landscape Connectivity & Conservation Planning

[Habitat fragmentation, wildlife corridors, and human-wildlife conflict, mapped and modeled.]{.eg-teaser-desc}

[Learn more →](services-geospatial.qmd#services){.eg-teaser-link}
:::

::: {.eg-teaser-card}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l4}

[LAYER 04]{.eg-layer-index}

### Social-Ecological Systems & Resilience Assessment

[Where land, climate, and community governance meet — analyzed together, not separately.]{.eg-teaser-desc}

[Learn more →](services-geospatial.qmd#services){.eg-teaser-link}
:::

::: {.eg-teaser-card}
[ ]{.eg-layer-toggle .eg-l5}

[LAYER 05]{.eg-layer-index}

### Climate Adaptation & Food-Security Modeling

[Drought, rangeland, and yield forecasting for the people who plan around them.]{.eg-teaser-desc}

[Learn more →](services-geospatial.qmd#services){.eg-teaser-link}
:::

:::
:::
:::

::: {#approach .eg-section}
::: {.eg-wrap}
::: {.eg-sec-head}
[Approach]{.eg-eyebrow}

## Three things every layer is built on.
:::

::: {.eg-approach-grid}
::: {.eg-approach-item}
[A]{.eg-num}

### Field-grounded

Every model is checked against ground truth — plots, transects, or partner verification — not just pixels.
:::

::: {.eg-approach-item}
[B]{.eg-num}

### Community-informed

Social-ecological methods mean local governance and knowledge shape the analysis, not just the output.
:::

::: {.eg-approach-item}
[C]{.eg-num}

### Registry-ready

Work is documented to the standard carbon registries, donors, and auditors actually require — no rework at review time.
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {#sectors .eg-section .eg-sectors}
::: {.eg-wrap}
::: {.eg-sec-head}
[Sectors]{.eg-eyebrow}

## Who we work with
:::

[Carbon & Conservation Finance]{.eg-sector-chip} [Government & County Planning]{.eg-sector-chip} [Multilaterals & Bilateral Donors]{.eg-sector-chip} [Research Institutions]{.eg-sector-chip} [Agriculture & Insurance]{.eg-sector-chip}
:::
:::

::: {#contact .eg-section .eg-contact}
::: {.eg-contact-wrap}
::: {.eg-contact-left}
## Have a landscape that needs a closer look?

Tell us what you're trying to prove, protect, or plan for. We'll tell you which layers apply.
:::

::: {.eg-contact-box}
[EMAIL]{.eg-mono-label}

[hello@equatorgeospatial.com](mailto:hello@equatorgeospatial.com){.eg-email}

[BASED IN]{.eg-mono-label}

[Nairobi, Kenya — serving East Africa]{.eg-email-sub}

[Send us a brief](mailto:hello@equatorgeospatial.com){.eg-btn .eg-btn-solid}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.eg-footer}
::: {.eg-footer-inner}
© 2026 EQUATOR GEOSPATIAL

NAIROBI, KENYA
:::
:::

:::
